DISCOVERY 612, DEFENDANT FEDERAL EXPRESS'S MOTION IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF IS ORDERED TO RESPOND TO FEDERAL EXPRESS'S SECOND SET OF INTERROGATORIES AND SECOND DEMAND FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS WITHIN 15 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. PLAINTIFF'S FAILURE TO RESPOND PREVIOUSLY WAS A MISUSE OF THE DISCOVERY PROCESS. SANCTIONS ARE AWARDED AGAINST PLAINTIFF AND HIS COUNSEL IN THE AMOUNT OF $300, TO BE PAID WITHIN 30 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. ORDER WAS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER, JR.

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Responses To Deft'S Second Set Of Special Interrogatories And Second Demand For Production Of Documents And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Aug-10-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
STANLEY M. WESSELS, ESQ., (415)956-4800, FOR DEFENDANT/MOVING PARTY
NO OTHER APPEARANCES WERE MADE

TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED AS FOLLOWS: Defendant Federal Express's motion is granted. Plaintiff is ordered to respond to Federal Express's second set of interrogatories and second demand for production of documents within 15 days of notice of this order. Plaintiff's failure to respond previously was a misuse of the discovery process. Sanctions are awarded against Plaintiff and his counsel in the amount of $300, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. ORDER WAS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER, JR. (RBU/612)
